programmes.show_description: Fun Times for Swingers: When Detective Inspector Jack Frost investigates the death of a local gigolo, he becomes interested in one of his clients for more than professional reasons.
Radio Control: Enduring sitcom. Technology takes over at the depot as a new radio control system is introduced, but Stan and Jack cause chaos as they get to grips with the airwaves.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
A Point of Honour: Classic British sitcom. Simon seeks an assistant, but he runs into trouble after interviewing an applicant and finds that discretion is the better part of valour.
Nowhere to Go: Enduring '70s sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor pal. Arthur's bike breaks down and threatens Stan's plans for a day out with the girls.
Laurel makes a drastic decision after arguing with her dad about Arthur. Shadrach talks to Greg about his experiences with Cain, and Pearl selects a winner for the cruise ticket.
Ashley and Laurel remain at loggerheads as Doug tries to smooth the waters between them. Andy is frustrated with Debbie's presence in Sarah's life and takes his anger out on Jo.
A social worker turns up and discovers Chesney living in squalor, while David's curiosity is aroused when he catches Gail and Tina talking in the kitchen.
Claire comes clean to Ashley about her actions as Maria comforts a devastated Kirk. Tina struggles to cope with her guilty conscience and Ryan considers getting to know Nick.
The Things We Do for Love: When a beautiful woman is found dead, the list of suspects leads Jack Frost into the heart of a religious community where someone is thinking impurely.
Living Off the Land: Joe and Rachel investigate a violent attack on a travelling hippy - but things are not quite what they seem - and David goes to war with a rival local taxi firm.
Guilty Secrets: Joe risks his career to prove the innocence of a fellow officer when a criminal allegation is made against them, and Bernie receives some staggering news.
Who Killed Cock Robin?: Feature-length drama. The disappearance of a villager brings DCI Tom Barnaby face to face with an old adversary who has reinvented himself as a local squire.
Viv confronts a witness found by her private investigator and Bob begins to doubt his wife. The Dingles steal the pub's furniture as Val prepares for the competition.
An anxious Tina prepares for her abortion, racked with guilt about keeping David in the dark. Tony makes Liam an offer and Norris is blamed for informing on Kirk.
Gail is forced to tell David about Tina's abortion, and in a fit of rage he pushes his mother down the stairs. Nick arrives in the pub to see Ryan, and Kirk visits Chesney.
Strike Up the Band: The police investigate when the rivalry between two brass bands leads to violence, and Dawn finds romance with a handsome stranger but her happiness may not last.
Return Crossing: A widow's life is endangered by the dodgy business dealings of her late husband, and the locals help a visitor on his romantic quest.
City Slickers: Comedy-drama series. Louisa deals with some new and very annoying neighbours, Mrs Tishell visits the Doc with a rash, and Joan and Bert have their cars keyed.
The Admirer: Comedy-drama series. Louisa is angry to discover that she has a rival for Martin's affections, and Joan finds that she has an admirer who is 30 years younger than her.
Striker: Crime drama. 1971 - Endeavour finds himself right back in the thick of it when an explosive murder at an Oxford college has potentially far-reaching political ramifications.
The Things We Do for Love: When a beautiful woman is found dead, the list of suspects leads Jack Frost into the heart of a religious community where someone is thinking impurely.
Foggy Night: Enduring '70s sitcom about a chirpy bus driver and his conductor. Stan has to pull off the road when freezing fog blankets the Home Counties and makes visibility nil.
Your daily escape designed to calm the mind and encourage relaxation and reflection.
Shopping from home.
All for Love: Detective drama series. Det Supt Charles Wycliffe faces a race against time to recapture a dangerous escaped prisoner.
The Trojan Horse: Wycliffe is sceptical about a man's distraught claims about his missing wife until a ransom note is found and a body is discovered at the wheel of a crashed car.
The Gift of Promise: When a businesswoman is bludgeoned to death, it looks like a case of blackmail gone wrong. But there is a decades-old secret at the heart of the case.
Greenshaw's Folly: Miss Marple helps a friend seek refuge at Greenshaw's Folly. But a shadow soon begins to fall over the various inhabitants living there.
The Theft of the Royal Ruby: Poirot is looking forward to Christmas on his own, but when the nation's security is threatened, he is called in to help track down a stolen ruby.
Ed Chamberlin presents coverage of racing from Sandown and Wolverhampton - the feature race is at Sandown with the big Imperial Cup handicap ahead of the 2026 Cheltenham Festival.
Things That Go Bump in the Night: The murder of an undertaker takes Barnaby and Scott to a village where residents are divided about the rights and wrongs of a spiritualist church.
The Blacktrees Prophecy: When a survivalist is found murdered in his bombproof shelter, Barnaby unravels a plot that reveals how far people will go to escape the end of the world.
The Debt of Lies: When the latest resident of a police retirement community is found dead, the tight-knit group regard a murder on their doorstep as both an affront and a challenge.
Darkwater: DCI Vera Stanhope must scrutinise the fractured relationships of a community when the body of a teenage boy is found in a reservoir in a remote rural town.
